%% Helper function to convert a point [X, Y, Z] into a comparable/hashable form if needed, 
%% but for small N, we will primarily rely on list equality checks in Erlang.
%% The point structure is [X, Y, Z].

%% Calculates the floor average midpoint between two points P1 and P2.
calculate_midpoint(P1, P2) ->
    [
        (P1 !! 0 + P2 !! 0) div 2,
        (P1 !! 1 + P2 !! 1) div 2,
        (P1 !! 2 + P2 !! 2) div 2
    ].

%% Generates all unique midpoint candidates from a list of points.
generate_candidates(AllPoints) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Candidates, P1) ->
        lists:foldl(fun(NewCandidates, P2) ->
            % Ensure we only pair distinct points (P1 /= P2)
            if P1 == P2 -> NewCandidates;
            true ->
                Candidate = calculate_midpoint(P1, P2),
                NewCandidates ++ [Candidate]
            end
        end, Candidates, AllPoints)
    end, [], AllPoints).

%% Recursive solver function.
%% k: current generation number (starting from 1 for the first generation step)
%% AllPoints: list of all unique points found up to generation k-1.
solve_recursive(k, AllPoints, Target) when k >= 1 ->
    % 1. Check if we have enough distinct points to generate new pairs
    if length(AllPoints) < 2 ->
        -1; % Cannot generate new points
    true ->
        % 2. Generate all candidates for generation k
        Candidates = generate_candidates(AllPoints),
        
        % 3. Filter for NovelPoints: points that are not already in AllPoints
        NovelPoints = lists:filter(fun(P) -> not lists:member(P, AllPoints) end, Candidates),
        
        % 4. Check for stagnation
        if length(NovelPoints) == 0 ->
            -1; % No new points were generated
        true ->
            % 5. Check if target was found in this generation k
            if lists:member(Target, NovelPoints) ->
                k; % Target found!
            true ->
                % 6. Update state and recurse
                NewAllPoints = AllPoints ++ NovelPoints,
                solve_recursive(k + 1, NewAllPoints, Target)
            end
        end
    end.

%% Main solver function implementation
solve(Points, Target) ->
    % 1. Preprocessing: Identify unique initial points and check for target in Gen 0
    InitialUniquePoints = lists:unique(Points),
    
    if lists:member(Target, InitialUniquePoints) ->
        0; % Target found in Generation 0
    true ->
        % 2. Start recursion for Generation 1
        solve_recursive(1, InitialUniquePoints, Target)
    end.
```
